nodes.x: THEN answered Bildad the Shuhite, and said,
CHAPTER VIII. Bildad showeth God's justice. PJVHEN answered Bildad: the: Shuhite, and said,
BILDAD THE SHUHITE... 1. Not much know about him 2. May have been a descendent of Shuah, son of Abraham and Keturah, who lived in the "east" (Gen 25:2,6)
Dominion and fear [are] with him, he maketh peace in his high places.
BILDAD'S FINAL RESPONSE (25:1-6) 1. He proclaims the greatness of God (25:1-3) a. Dominion and fear belong to Him, He makes peace in His high places b. His armies are innumerable
» And when he smileth at thee he is probing thee®®. τ ruthless one maketh peace, While plotting against the life of many’.
Is there any number of his armies? and upon whom doth not his light arise?
How then can man be justified with God? or how can he be clean [that is] born of a woman?
Behold even to the moon, and it shineth not; yea, the stars are not pure in his sight.
How much less man, [that is] a worm? and the son of man, [which is] a worm?

nodes.lb: Jewish Thought: Duties of the Heart, Seventh Treatise on Repentance, Introduction 6
nodes.x: Among them, what the verse says: "for the imagination of man's heart is evil from his youth" (Bereishis 8:21), and "the L-ord saw that the wickedness of man was great in the earth, and that every imagination of the thoughts of his heart was only evil continually" (Bereishis 6:5), and "a man is born as a wild donkey's colt" (Iyov 11:12), and "Behold, the moon has no brightness"(Iyov 25:5) and "how…
